channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Treasury Hack: Lawmakers Seek More Details on Scope and Third-Party Vulnerabilities
item.description: Lawmakers are seeking more details from the Treasury Department regarding a recent China-connected cyber breach, including potential vulnerabilities in a third-party vendor.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: My 2025 tech resolutions: ditching X, avoiding AI, and more
item.description: So many of 2024’s tech plot twists defied predictions, but we should have seen one thing coming: The industry would once again pivot away from learning from its mistakes.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: A double dose of nasty winter is about to smack much of the US with snow, ice and biting cold
item.description: Meteorologists forecast a strong snow and ice storm followed by brutally cold conditions will soon smack the eastern two-thirds of the United States.
item.dc:creator: By Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US PC Market Set for Slowdown in 2025 Amid Proposed Tariffs and Windows Upgrade Costs
item.description: Canalys predicts US PC market to slow down in 2025 due to proposed Trump tariffs and Windows 11 upgrade costs, which could increase PC prices by as much as 46%.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows 11 24H2 is in danger of becoming hated by gamers
item.description: Windows 11’s 24H2 update has hit gamers with quite a number of bugs, and now Path of Exile 2 players are being made miserable.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Government-imposed internet shutdowns cost over $7 billion in 2024 – and Pakistan was the most affected nation
item.description: While not the most costly year, 2024 saw the highest number of countries affected and blockage hours. Here's what's at stake for 2025.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of snoopy eavesdropping
item.description: The lawsuit alleges the tech giant made recordings even when people didn’t seek to activate the virtual assistant with the trigger words, “Hey, Siri.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Latest Nvidia RTX 5000 power usage rumors make me scared that my PSU will be nowhere near enough for the RTX 5080
item.description: I’m likely going to have to stick with an RTX 5070 for my graphics card upgrade – or go AMD RDNA 4 instead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple denies wrongdoing but settles to pay $95 million cash (about $20 per class member) in Siri privacy suit
item.description: Apple agreed to pay $95 million in cash to settle a proposed class action lawsuit claiming that its voice-activated Siri assistant violated users’ privacy. A preliminary settlement was filed on Tuesday night in the Oakland, California federal court, and requires approval by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: I’m a techie, but I’ve fallen for paper notes again
item.description: Usually I type my initial thoughts for these columns on a computer, but this week I wrote them in a paper notebook instead. I’ve never been much of a paper person.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: What is Turo? Car rental app used in New Orleans and Las Vegas
item.description: Turo, a San Francisco-based company founded in 2010, operates differently from traditional car rental services.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes
item.description: AI tools are accelerating the development of deepfake schemes and disinformation campaigns that led to fraud The IRS is trying to keep up. The post IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry
item.description: Our government needs to maintain trust through competence, and that competence will increasingly be about powering government services with AI. The post The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: 44 awesome Android app discoveries from 2024
item.description: It’s the calm before the storm. Today, on New Year’s Day, we have a brief moment to pause and prepare — and set ourselves up for success. From a tech perspective, that means taking the time to clean up and optimize your smartphone setup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The best new Windows 11 features of 2024
item.description: Microsoft is a little sneaky. Sure, there’s just one “big” update for Windows 11 each year. But Microsoft’s Windows team is always working on something, and new Windows 11 features are arriving on PCs every month — even outside of those high-profile updates.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hands-on with Adobe’s Acrobat AI Assistant: AI for your PDFs
item.description: We may be on the brink of 2025, but PDFs are still unavoidable in the professional world. No matter what industry you work in, you’re bound to whittle away precious moments wading through reports, white papers, and other dense documents in that clunky-feeling form.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: These 6 tech questions were settled in 2024
item.description: The Silicon Valley hype cycle follows a familiar pattern: an emerging technology or tech product or service is hinted at, rumored, leaked, reported on, announced, and then shipped. That’s the cycle for what actually happens.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Multitenant data management with TiDB
item.description: In a previous article, we explored various approaches to managing data for multitenant applications. We examined the benefits, compromises and limitations of different deployment models, including share-nothing, share-everything, and hybrid.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The future of open source will be messy
item.description: Open Source Initiative (OSI) chief Stefano Maffulli says Meta is “bullying” the industry on the concept of open source. The Economist more colorfully characterizes the dispute as “bare-it-all purists … confronting Meta, the social-media giant controlled by a mankini-clad Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple Mail app redesign introduces new categorization feature in iOS 18.2
item.description: In iOS 18.2 and later, Apple's Mail app enhances email management by automatically sorting messages into distinct categories to help find emails faster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
